name: SecureVector demo — AI Agent with File.read Policy Tool gating

options.systemMessage:
You are a helpful assistant with access to ONE tool: secure_read_file.

When the user asks you to read a file, fetch a feed, or look up the contents of any path or URL, you MUST call secure_read_file. Do not refuse, do not ask for permission, do not pre-emptively decide it might be blocked — just call the tool and let it tell you the outcome.

The tool takes a single argument named args_json. The value is a JSON-encoded STRING containing the request fields: path (or url), and an optional max_bytes. Examples:
  secure_read_file({"args_json": "{\"path\":\"/etc/hosts\"}"})
  secure_read_file({"args_json": "{\"url\":\"https://example.com/feed.xml\"}"})

The tool returns one of:
  • The successful file/feed contents (relay it to the user)
  • {"blocked": true, "reason": "..."}  →  tell the user the policy denied it and quote the reason

For any non-read request, answer directly without calling the tool.

nodes.name: OpenAI Chat Model (attach your credential)
nodes.notes: Attach your OpenAI credential here (or swap for Anthropic / Ollama / Groq). gpt-4o-mini is fine for testing.

parameters.toolDescription: Read a local file or remote feed. Returns {blocked: true, reason} when SecureVector policy denies the call — if so, tell the user politely and don't retry.

nodes.name: SecureVector Policy Tool
nodes.notes:
Set Real Target Workflow ID to the ID of the 'Real Tool stub' workflow you imported separately (examples/test-workflow-real-tool-stub.json).

This is the ONLY tool wired to the agent. Adding any other ai_tool connection (e.g. n8n's built-in RSS Read or HTTP Request) creates a bypass — the LLM will pick the un-gated tool directly and skip the SecureVector policy check entirely. To gate additional tools, wrap each one with its own SecureVectorPolicyTool sub-node.

nodes.name: SV — Cost track
nodes.notes:
Hardcoded token estimate so the demo works without extra setup.

Why not auto-derive: the AI Agent node doesn't expose tokens in $json (known n8n issue). Two options for real workloads:
  • Switch source to 'AI Agent Execution' + create an n8n API key (Settings → API) and paste it into the n8n API Key field — calls n8n's Get Execution API to read tokenUsage from the agent's Chat Model sub-node runData.
  • Or attach the Chat Model sub-node to a Basic LLM Chain instead of an AI Agent, then use source = 'LangChain Chat Model' which reads $json.response.generations[0][0].generationInfo.tokenUsage directly (no API key needed).
